name: research-jtbd-interviews
description: Run switch interviews and extract the four Forces of Progress when the user wants to understand why people actually buy.

name: research-audience-discovery
description: Turn raw interview material into JTBD segment cards the team can write to, price to, and channel to.

name: research-competitive-scan
description: Map the customer's real competitor set (including non-consumption and workarounds) before positioning, pricing, or hero copy.

name: copy-customer-voice
description: Mine raw customer language (reviews, tickets, calls) before drafting any headline, hero, CTA, or email.

name: copy-awareness-stages
description: Diagnose the reader's awareness stage and pick the right first line for headlines, hooks, ad opens, and email subjects.

name: copy-hook-craft
description: Write scroll-stopping openers — social first lines, video opens, subject lines, cold-outbound first sentences, ad headlines.

name: sales-discovery-call
description: Structure a SPIN-disciplined discovery call so the buyer surfaces their own problem, cost, and need-payoff before any pitch.

name: sales-objection-handling
description: Sort stated vs. real objections across price, timing, authority, fit, and trust — and respond by acknowledge-ask-respond, not argue.

name: sales-close-and-next-step
description: Distinguish advancement from continuation and propose concrete, achievable, mutually-agreed next steps that actually move deals.

name: forge-value-pricing
description: Value-based pricing — willingness-to-pay research, value-equation math, and the three-strategy decision (premium/value-capture/penetration).

name: forge-offer-construction
description: Assemble the offer — outcome language, bonus-pairs-anxiety, guarantee, and the offer stack that earns the price.

name: forge-packaging-tiers
description: Three-tier packaging (good/better/best), decoy pricing, the upgrade ladder, and when to skip tiers entirely.

name: mira-brand-foundation
description: Build the onlyness statement — Neumeier's difference-beats-betterness positioning before any visual decision.

name: mira-visual-system
description: Lock the visual system — type, color, voice — so recognition compounds across every touchpoint.

name: mira-presentation-design
description: Design presentation visuals — decks, hero blocks, and landing visuals — anchored to the onlyness statement.

name: beacon-channel-strategy
description: Pick the channel mix using See/Think/Do/Care — stage-first, not platform-first.

name: beacon-seo-organic
description: Organic search strategy — intent-stage keyword mapping, content gap audit, and what to write next.

name: beacon-paid-acquisition
description: Paid channels — Binet/Field 60/40 brand-vs-activation split, attribution discipline, and when to scale.

name: beacon-instagram
description: Instagram — content format mix, Reels-first reality, and the carousel-vs-Reel-vs-Story decision.

name: beacon-linkedin
description: LinkedIn — personal vs company-page strategy, post format mix, and DM-to-meeting motion.

name: beacon-email-sequences
description: Email sequence design — welcome, nurture, sales, win-back. Cadence, segmentation, and inbox-deliverability.

name: beacon-short-form-video
description: Short-form video — TikTok/Reels/Shorts mechanics, hook-first structure, and platform-native repurposing.

name: beacon-twitter-x
description: Twitter/X — feed mechanics, thread structure, and reply-discoverability strategy.

name: beacon-facebook
description: Facebook — page/group/ads ecosystem and where it still earns attention.

name: beacon-podcast
description: Podcast — show structure, guest economy, and distribution-as-marketing.

name: beacon-newsletter
description: Newsletter — format, cadence, monetization paths from sponsorship to product.

name: beacon-affiliate-program
description: Affiliate program design — payout structure, partner recruitment, and tracking infrastructure.

name: beacon-blog-content
description: Blog content strategy — cluster topics, SEO-aware writing, and the publish-and-distribute loop.

name: beacon-funnel-build
description: Sales funnel build — landing page, opt-in, nurture, conversion event, retention loop.

name: patch-operating-rhythm
description: Install the operating rhythm — daily standup, weekly review, monthly KPI, quarterly priorities. Harnish's Scaling Up cadence.

name: patch-crm-hygiene
description: Keep the CRM honest — stale-deal audits, contact-data hygiene, pipeline-stage discipline.

name: patch-process-design
description: Turn chaos into SOPs — find the failure mode, document the fix, name the owner.

name: spark-curriculum-architecture
description: Backward-design a course or book from the learner transformation. Outcomes first, modules second.

name: spark-long-form-narrative
description: Build the long-form arc — chapter logic, narrative tension, and the through-line that holds a 100-page work together.

name: spark-learner-engagement
description: Design for completion — Fogg's tiny habits, spaced repetition, and the activation moments learners actually need.

name: coin-runway-and-burn
description: Runway math — burn rate, cash-out date, and the kill-line that triggers a hard reset.

name: coin-unit-economics
description: CAC, LTV, contribution margin, and payback period — the four numbers that determine whether the math works.

name: coin-pricing-math
description: The numbers behind a pricing decision — gross-margin floor, price-sensitivity calc, and what changes for each tier.

name: sentry-formation-and-structure
description: Entity formation — LLC vs C-corp vs S-corp, ownership structure, cap-table basics. Not legal advice; flags when you need a lawyer.

name: sentry-contracts-and-terms
description: Contract starter pack — mutual NDA, MSA, ToS, privacy policy, contractor agreement. Not legal advice; flags when you need a lawyer.

name: sentry-ip-and-compliance
description: IP basics + compliance — trademark, copyright, GDPR, AI rules, consumer protection. Not legal advice; flags when you need a lawyer.

name: stage-pitch-deck
description: Build the 10-12 slide investor deck — Sequoia structure reordered through Raskin's shift sentence.

name: stage-narrative-shift
description: Name the macro-trend shift that makes this company inevitable. The shift-sentence pass/fail before any deck draft.

name: stage-demo-day-and-q-a
description: Demo day mechanics + the typical 20 investor questions. Rehearsal-ready, not slide-ware.

name: helm-decision-frames
description: Founder decision frames — first principles, inversion, second-order thinking, pre-mortem.

name: helm-founder-cadence
description: Weekly review, light OKRs, prioritization stack. The personal operating rhythm that compounds.

name: helm-stuck-and-unstuck
description: Diagnose the kind of stuck (call / work / question) and name the three options: push, walk away, or ask for help.

name: probe-fake-door-tests
description: Smoke tests, landing-page tests, sign-up-intent measurement — the cheapest way to disprove a hypothesis.

name: probe-mvp-design
description: The smallest thing that could disprove the hypothesis. Ries's build-measure-learn applied to the first cycle.

name: probe-validation-rubric
description: When has it validated? When has it failed? Falsifiable thresholds and the kill criteria that protect you from sunk cost.

name: vault-storefront-foundation
description: PDP architecture, listing structure, Shopify+ fundamentals. Traffic-source-first PDP design.

name: vault-agentic-geo
description: Generative Engine Optimization — optimizing for AI shoppers, ChatGPT discovery, agentic-storefront mechanics.

name: vault-marketplace-ops
description: Amazon PPC + inventory rhythm + review velocity. Marketplace-native operating discipline.

name: smith-shape-and-spec
description: Shape Up shaping + spec writing for coding agents. Appetite, breadboard, rabbit-holes, no-gos.

name: smith-architecture-decisions
description: ADR pattern, tradeoff tables, regret-check analysis. What you'd kick yourself for in 18 months.

name: smith-agent-handoff
description: Write specs and tickets that Cursor / Claude Code / coding agents can execute reliably — with kill switches and verification.

name: mend-ticket-triage
description: P0-P3 classification, response templates, escalation matrix. Murphy's Desired Outcome framing.

name: mend-onboarding-flow
description: The activation path — Desired Outcome, the magic moment, and the first 30-day journey.

name: mend-churn-prevention
description: Churn signals, save-call playbooks, expansion-conversation triggers. Save-or-grow before the renewal.

name: lens-funnel-diagnosis
description: Where the funnel drops, what to fix, and the minimum sample size before you call meaning on it.

name: lens-cohort-and-retention
description: Cohort curves, magic-moment analysis, retention math that distinguishes survivorship from signal.

name: lens-north-star-and-experiments
description: Pick the North Star metric and design Kohavi-grade experiments — pre-registration, MDE, guardrails.

name: verdict-score-and-rank
description: Score content on 8 dimensions (hook, clarity, emotional pull, differentiation, value density, voice, shareability, platform fit).

name: verdict-flag-and-fix
description: Catch AI-mush, vague claims, clickbait-without-payoff, drag, missing CTA — the editorial smoke alarms.

name: verdict-the-one-edit
description: Name the single specific edit that would make this 20% better. Not a list — the one.

name: copy-lens-curiosity-packaging
description: Curiosity & packaging lens — MrBeast retention discipline applied to title, thumbnail, opening.

name: copy-lens-value-framing
description: Pain & value lens — Hormozi's value-equation framing applied to hook and CTA angle.

name: copy-lens-clarity
description: Education & clarity lens — Abdaal/Feynman simplicity. Best analogy, common mistake, clarity score.

name: copy-lens-positioning
description: Authority & belief reframe lens — Welsh's solopreneur authority playbook for sharper positioning.

name: copy-lens-business-angle
description: Money & ownership lens — Sanchez's boring-business framing for time/money impact and shareability.

name: copy-lens-storytelling
description: Narrative & emotion lens — Bartlett-style diary storytelling. Tension, before, turning point, after.

name: copy-lens-differentiation
description: Remarkable & contrarian lens — Godin's purple-cow tradition. Obvious version, differentiated angle, compressed insight.

name: copy-lens-platform-native
description: Attention & platform-native lens — Gary Vee's day-trading attention applied to platform-specific opening line.

name: slate-role-design
description: Define a role as a hypothesis — name the outcome that stops being delivered when the seat is empty.

name: slate-candidate-evaluation
description: Structured interviews, signal-vs-noise, reference-check discipline. Decision in 3 weeks, not 3 months.

name: slate-hiring-structure
description: Contractor vs employee vs fractional vs agency — and the comp structure that fits the work.

name: voiceprint-voice-interview
description: The 22-question Interviewer phase — adaptive questioning that pushes back on vague answers and captures audience, refusals, voice, structure, influences, stance.

name: voiceprint-voice-compile
description: The Compiler phase — synthesize samples + transcripts + answers into a structured 6-section voice profile. Samples beat self-report; flag the gap when they contradict.

name: voiceprint-voice-maintenance
description: The 6-month refresh logic + voice-notes.md running log. When to update, when not, and how the daily delete-the-AI-tic habit becomes next quarter's refresh input.

name: xrole-consume-voice-profile
description: Cross-role mode skill — teaches Copy/Spark/Stage/Mira how to load a Voiceprint-produced voice profile and draft in the user's voice instead of generic AI.

name: sentry-employment-and-classification
description: Employment law receiving-end — W2-vs-1099 classification, at-will basics, equity-as-comp tax exposure. Not legal advice; escalates fast.

name: coin-hire-affordability
description: The math behind "can I afford this hire?" — fully-loaded cost, labor-efficiency-ratio floor, payback window.

name: lens-marketing-attribution
description: Marketing attribution — last-click / first-click / linear / time-decay, the small-data attribution trap, why every platform overclaims.

name: research-market-research
description: Macro market sizing — TAM/SAM/SOM, industry-cycle assessment, the bottom-up beats top-down rule. User-toggle (most users don't need this).

name: sales-partnerships
description: Partnership / BD discipline — partner-fit rules, the three partnership shapes (referral / co-marketing / integration), the partnership-debt trap.

name: humanizer-tell-detector
description: Diagnose AI-tells in a draft — score perplexity, burstiness, and structural tells; flag specific lines; recommend rewrite or pass.

name: humanizer-rewrite-pass
description: Full humanize rewrite — vocabulary substitution from the AI blacklist + sentence-length variation (Klinkenborg's short-long-short) + parenthetical asides. Both axes together.

name: humanizer-voice-match
description: Layer the user's Voiceprint profile on top of a rewrite pass — applies DO/DON'T rules from the voice file so the output is humanized AND sounds like the user, not just like a generic person.
